subject: Re: Warp Crashing
body:
Thanks for the suggestions. I've attached the lastcrash file in the program 
directory but the timestamp is from before the most recent crash. Should 
there also be a lastcrash file in the directory that I am running Warp in? 
If so I do not see one. Looking at the error it seems like it might be 
related to the connection to our network mount. I'll give the fix a try. 

Cheers,
Evan

8/16/2018 10:57:50 PM:
System.AggregateException: A Task's exception(s) were not observed either 
by Waiting on the Task or accessing its Exception property. As a result, 
the unobserved exception was rethrown by the finalizer thread. ---> 
System.IO.IOException: An unexpected network error occurred.

   at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
   at System.IO.FileStream.WriteCore(Byte[] buffer, Int32 offset, Int32 
count)
   at Warp.Tools.IOHelper.WriteMapFloat(String path, MapHeader header, 
Single[][] data)
   at Warp.Image.WriteMRC(String path, Single pixelSize, Boolean 
doStatistics, HeaderMRC header)
   at Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass229_0.<ExportMovie>b__2()
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.Execute()
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
---> (Inner Exception #0) System.IO.IOException: An unexpected network 
error occurred.

   at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
   at System.IO.FileStream.WriteCore(Byte[] buffer, Int32 offset, Int32 
count)
   at Warp.Tools.IOHelper.WriteMapFloat(String path, MapHeader header, 
Single[][] data)
   at Warp.Image.WriteMRC(String path, Single pixelSize, Boolean 
doStatistics, HeaderMRC header)
   at Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass229_0.<ExportMovie>b__2()
   at System.Threading.Tasks.Task.Execute()<---


On Saturday, August 25, 2018 at 12:43:04 PM UTC-7, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hi Evan,
>
> does the crash leave a message in LastCrash.txt in the Warp folder? A 
> display-related bug slipped into the 1.0.5 release, although it's unlikely 
> to cause the behavior you describe. On the other hand, failure to process 
> an item should result in it getting deselected instead of a full crash. Here 
> is a fix <https://www.dropbox.com/s/l3i9can1vw3jf1c/Warp105fix.zip?dl=0> 
> you can try.
>
> Individual items can be hard to find in large projects, indeed. I'm 
> working on better navigation for 1.0.6.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
>
> On Friday, August 24, 2018 at 10:31:11 PM UTC+2, gre...@gene.com 
> <javascript:> wrote:
>>
>> Hi Dimitry,
>>
>> I've been having trouble with Warp crashing recently. I have a large data 
>> collection that is currently on-going. Part way through the data collection 
>> our RAID array filled up causing SerialEM to write out tif files without 
>> any data which I think are causing the program to crash. I've removed the 
>> bad tif files from the Warp directly and their associated xml files but I'm 
>> still getting intermittent crashes. I notice when I start Warp after the 
>> crash there is a single "Outdated" image that I imagine might be causing 
>> the issue. The program does not crash right away when I start processing 
>> again. Is their an easy way to identify the outdated image (it's a large 
>> collection so it's difficult to see the individual yellow dot). How does 
>> the program currently deal with "bad" files that it is trying to process? I 
>> imagine if I restart a new directly with only the good images it'll work 
>> fine as in the past but I wanted to share this information in case it is 
>> useful for future releases. If you have any suggestions I'd be happy to try 
>> them out. 
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Evan
>>
>
